steps.title: Identify what the question requires
steps.content: The student wants to compare the causes of crown shyness proposed by both theories. A correct answer must state Jacobs's specific cause (branches brushing and breaking) and Ng's specific cause (branches detecting shade and stopping growth), using language that contrasts them.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• "While Jacobs proposes that crown shyness is caused by neighboring tree branches brushing against one another, Ng posits that it occurs when branches detect shade from nearby trees' branches.": Names both causes explicitly and uses a contrast structure ("While...Ng posits") to compare them directly. ✓
  • "Both Jacobs and Ng have proposed theories to explain what causes crown shyness.": Only acknowledges that two theories exist; gives no information about what either theory proposes as a cause. ✗
  • "Ng posits the mutual shade avoidance theory, whereas Jacobs proposes an alternative theory.": Names Ng's theory by title and vaguely calls Jacobs's an "alternative" — neither specific cause is compared. ✗
  • "Jacobs's mutual abrasion theory proposes that when neighboring trees brush against one another, branches break off, resulting in a phenomenon in which the tops of trees grow close together but don't overlap.": Explains only Jacobs's theory; Ng's theory and cause are entirely absent. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
